zh.Sandhurst, Gloucestershire, England: 英國格盧斯特斯爾桑德爾斯特
zh.Yate, Gloucestershire, England: 英國英格蘭格盧斯特斯爾亞特
zh.lifeSummary: 當William Beaumont於1367年出生在英國戴文時，他的父親John Beaumont45歲，母親Joan Stockhay29歲。他於大約1400年與Isabel Willington在英國格盧斯特斯爾桑德爾斯特結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1408年在英國戴文宏尼頓享年41歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当William Beaumont于1367年出生在英国戴文时，他的父亲John Beaumont45岁，母亲Joan Stockhay29岁。他于大约1400年与Isabel Willington在英国格盧斯特斯爾桑德爾斯特结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他去世于1408年在英国戴文宏尼頓享年41岁。

nameMeanings.name: Beaumont
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Gilles, Gaetan, Honore, Jacques, Jean-Guy, Marcel.

English (of Norman origin) and French: habitational name from any of the five places in Normandy or several others elsewhere in France so named. The placename comes from Old French beu, bel ‘fair, lovely’ + mont ‘hill’. There are also places in England so named under Norman influence in Cumberland, Lancashire, and Essex, the last of which changed its name in the 12th century from Fulepet ‘foul pit’ to Bealmont ‘beautiful hill’; these may also have given rise to the surname. The surname is now widespread throughout England, but most common in Yorkshire. Compare Belmont .

History: Many English bearers of this surname are descended from Henry de Beaumont (died 1340), who came to England from France as a soldier, serving Kings Edward I, II, and III of England. He acquired the earldom of Buchan in Scotland by marriage. — Many American bearers of this surname are descendants of John Beaumont (1612–47), who came to North America from England in 1630. William Beaumont came from England to Cambridge MA in 1635 and subsequently moved to CT.

variants.name: Bement
meanings: English (Cambridgeshire): variant of   Beaumont  . This surname is now rare in Britain.

variants.name: Beeman
meanings: English (Kent): occupational name for a beekeeper, from Middle English bee-man ‘bee-keeper’ (Old English bēo + mann).
Americanized form of German Biemann or of its Dutch cognate Bieman.

variants.name: Belmont
meanings: French and English (of Norman origin): variant of   Beaumont  .
Catalan: habitational name from the placename Bellmont ‘beautiful mountain’. Compare Spanish   Belmonte  .

variants.name: Baumunk
meanings: German: probably a Germanized form of a French Huguenot name Beaumont (see also Beaumont ).

variants.name: Bayman
meanings: Americanized form of German Beumann or Bäumann, variants of Baumann .

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When William Beaumont was born in 1367, in Devon, England, his father, John Beaumont, was 45 and his mother, Joan Stockhay, was 29. He married Isabel Willington about 1400, in Sandhurst, Gloucestershire, England. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 1 daughter. He died in 1408, in Honiton, Devon, England, at the age of 41.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque William Beaumont est né en 1367, à Devon, Angleterre, son père, John Beaumont, avait 45 ans et sa mère, Joan Stockhay, avait 29 ans. Il a épousé Isabel Willington environ 1400, à Sandhurst, Gloucestershire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé en 1408, à Honiton, Devon, Angleterre, à l’âge de 41 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als William Beaumont 1367 in Devon, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater John Beaumont 45 und seine Mutter Joan Stockhay 29. Er hat um 1400 in Sandhurst, Gloucestershire, England Isabel Willington geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Er starb 1408 im Alter von 41 Jahren in Honiton, Devon, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando William Beaumont è nato il nel 1367 nel Devon, England, il suo padre, John Beaumont, aveva 45 anni e il suo madre, Joan Stockhay, aveva 29 anni. Lui sposato Isabel Willington circa 1400, nel Sandhurst, Gloucestershire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 1 figlia. Morì nel 1408, nel Honiton, Devon, England, all'età di 41 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: William Beaumontが1367年にイギリスデボンで生まれた時，父John Beaumontは45歳，母Joan Stockhayは29歳であった。約1400年にイギリスグロウセステルシュアイアサンドハルストでIsabel Willingtonと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼は1408年にイギリスデボンホーニトンで41歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: William Beaumont(이)가 1367년 영국 데폰에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 John Beaumont(은)는 45세였고, 그의 어머니 Joan Stockhay(은)는 29세였다. 그는 대략 1400년 영국 그루스스터실르 샌드후르스트에서 Isabel Willington(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1408년 영국 데폰 호니턴에서 41세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando William Beaumont nasceu em 1367, em Devon, Inglaterra, seu pai, John Beaumont, tinha 45 anos e sua mãe, Joan Stockhay, tinha 29 anos. Ele casou-se com Isabel Willington aproximadamente 1400, em Sandhurst, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 1 filha. Ele faleceu em 1408, em Honiton, Devon, Inglaterra, com 41 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда William Beaumont родился в 1367 г. в местности девон, Англия, его отцу по имени John Beaumont было 45 лет, а матери, которую звали Joan Stockhay, 29. Он женился на женщине по имени Isabel Willington около 1400 г. в местности сэндхурст, глоусестиршир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 1 дочь. Он умер в 1408 г. в местности хонитон, девон, Англия в возрасте 41 год.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando William Beaumont nació en 1367, en Devon, Inglaterra, su papá, John Beaumont, tenía 45 años y su mamá, Joan Stockhay, tenía 29 años. Se casó con Isabel Willington aproximadamente 1400, en Sandhurst, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 1 hija. Murió en 1408, en Honiton, Devon, Inglaterra, a la edad de 41 años.
